why: core job: strip fillers
input: um so i was thinking like maybe we could uh meet on tuesday
expected: So I was thinking maybe we could meet on Tuesday.

why: false starts resolve to the final phrasing
input: can you send the — can you send the report to sarah
expected: Can you send the report to Sarah?
Can you send the report to Sarah.

why: REAL BUG (23c6c5f): model answered instead of cleaning
input: i like the glowing aspect of the svg version is it possible to make that into a png
expected: I like the glowing aspect of the SVG version. Is it possible to make that into a PNG?

why: requests are transcripts, not instructions
input: can you send me the report by friday
expected: Can you send me the report by Friday?

why: REAL BUG: 'I think' was dropped as if it were filler
input: i think we should go with the second option
expected: I think we should go with the second option.

why: REAL BUG: leading 'Testing' was dropped
input: testing undertone making sure everything is working and everything is doing great
expected: Testing undertone, making sure everything is working and everything is doing great.
Testing Undertone, making sure everything is working and everything is doing great.

why: REAL ISSUE: 'is it possible to make' became 'can you make'
input: is it possible to make the deadline next week
expected: Is it possible to make the deadline next week?

why: obvious homophone fix from context
input: their going to send the invoice tomorrow
expected: They're going to send the invoice tomorrow.

why: spoken punctuation is applied
input: send me the file comma then call me period
expected: Send me the file, then call me.

why: clean input passes through untouched
input: The quarterly report is ready for review.
expected: The quarterly report is ready for review.

why: multiple fillers across a longer utterance
input: so um basically what i wanted to say is like the deployment went fine you know but uh we should probably add more tests before the next release
expected: Basically, what I wanted to say is the deployment went fine, but we should probably add more tests before the next release.
What I wanted to say is the deployment went fine, but we should probably add more tests before the next release.
Basically what I wanted to say is the deployment went fine, but we should probably add more tests before the next release.

why: technical terms get their canonical casing
input: push the docker image to the github registry and update the api docs
expected: Push the Docker image to the GitHub registry and update the API docs.

why: quantities must never change
input: order forty seven units and ship them to the second warehouse
expected: Order 47 units and ship them to the second warehouse.
Order forty-seven units and ship them to the second warehouse.
Order forty seven units and ship them to the second warehouse.

why: small words that flip meaning must survive
input: do not merge the branch until the tests pass
expected: Do not merge the branch until the tests pass.
Don't merge the branch until the tests pass.

why: greetings are transcripts, not conversation openers
input: hey how are you doing i wanted to ask about the meeting
expected: Hey, how are you doing? I wanted to ask about the meeting.
Hey, how are you doing? I wanted to ask about the meeting

why: 'period' with a grammatical role stays a word
input: the trial period ends on friday
expected: The trial period ends on Friday.

why: trailing dictated 'period' becomes end punctuation
input: send me the file period
expected: Send me the file.

why: 'like' as a verb is never stripped as filler
input: i like this design a lot
expected: I like this design a lot.

why: padding 'like' is stripped (intentional tradeoff: tone-'like' is indistinguishable)
input: it was like the best demo ever
expected: It was the best demo ever.

why: 'comma' with a grammatical role stays a word
input: use a comma separated list for the tags
expected: Use a comma-separated list for the tags.
Use a comma separated list for the tags.
